agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Digital wallet platform enabling peer-to-peer money transfers with tiered identity verification, regulatory compliance (KYC/AML), and account security controls. The product operates in a regulated financial services space where compliance failures carry legal and financial penalties.","bounded_context":"Digital Wallet — Transfers & Account Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"KYC (Know Your Customer)","definition":"Identity verification process that determines an account's verification tier and governs what actions the account holder may perform."},{"term":"Verified Account","definition":"An account that has completed full KYC identity verification. May send, receive, and withdraw funds up to the daily transfer limit of $5,000."},{"term":"Partially-Verified Account","definition":"An account that has completed some but not all KYC steps. May send funds up to $500/day and receive funds, but specific capabilities (e.g., withdrawal) are governed by the unverified rules unless otherwise stated."},{"term":"Unverified Account","definition":"An account with no identity verification completed. May receive funds only — cannot send or withdraw."},{"term":"Settlement","definition":"The finalization of a transaction, occurring at T+1 (one business day after initiation). Once settled, a transaction is irreversible."},{"term":"T+1","definition":"Settlement window: one business day after the transaction is initiated."},{"term":"Sanctioned Entity","definition":"A person, organization, or jurisdiction on a government-maintained sanctions list (e.g., OFAC SDN list). Transfers to or from sanctioned entities must be blocked."},{"term":"Daily Transfer Limit","definition":"The maximum cumulative amount a user may send in a rolling 24-hour (or calendar-day) period, determined by verification tier."},{"term":"Account Lock","definition":"A security state triggered by 5 consecutive failed login attempts. A locked account cannot be accessed until a support agent manually unlocks it."},{"term":"High-Value Transaction","definition":"Any transaction exceeding $1,000 that triggers a mandatory notification to the account holder."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"An account must complete full KYC identity verification before it is permitted to send money.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing unverified users to send funds violates regulatory requirements (AML/KYC). The system is in a corrupt compliance state if this is bypassed.","testable_assertion":"Any send-money request from an account with verification status != VERIFIED or PARTIALLY_VERIFIED must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Unverified accounts may receive money but cannot send or withdraw funds.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a regulatory constraint — permitting sends or withdrawals from unverified accounts violates compliance obligations and exposes the platform to legal liability.","testable_assertion":"Given an account with status UNVERIFIED, send and withdraw operations must return a rejection; receive operations must succeed."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Daily transfer limit for fully verified accounts is $5,000.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The $5,000 threshold is a business policy that could be adjusted by product or compliance leadership without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers from a verified account within the daily window must not exceed $5,000."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Daily transfer limit for partially-verified accounts is $500.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The $500 threshold is a tiered business policy, adjustable without system corruption.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers from a partially-verified account within the daily window must not exceed $500."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A transaction cannot be reversed once it has been settled (T+1).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Reversing a settled transaction corrupts the financial ledger and violates the integrity of the settlement system. Post-settlement corrections must use separate compensating transactions (refunds/chargebacks), not reversals.","testable_assertion":"Any reversal request for a transaction with status SETTLED must be rejected. Only transactions in PENDING or INITIATED status (pre-settlement) may be reversed."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked automatically before execution.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Processing a transaction to a sanctioned entity violates federal law (e.g., OFAC regulations). This is a legal invariant — if violated, the platform faces criminal liability.","testable_assertion":"Any transfer where the recipient matches a sanctioned-entity list must be blocked before funds move, and the attempt must be logged for compliance audit."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Five consecutive failed login attempts must lock the account.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a security integrity constraint. If the system fails to lock after 5 consecutive failures, it is in a vulnerable state that could lead to unauthorized access to financial accounts.","testable_assertion":"After exactly 5 consecutive failed login attempts, the account status must transition to LOCKED. A successful login resets the counter."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"A locked account can only be unlocked by a support agent — not by the user, not by a timer, not by any automated process.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing self-service or automated unlock after a brute-force lockout defeats the security purpose. Only a human-verified support interaction should restore access.","testable_assertion":"An account in LOCKED status must remain LOCKED until a support agent explicitly performs an unlock action. No user-facing or automated pathway may transition the account out of LOCKED status."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"A notification must be sent for any transaction exceeding $1,000.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The $1,000 notification threshold is a product policy. Failure to notify is a UX degradation, not a data corruption or compliance violation. The threshold is adjustable.","testable_assertion":"When a transaction with amount > $1,000 is initiated, a notification event must be emitted to the account holder."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An unverified account with a balance of $200","when":"The account holder attempts to send $50 to another user","then":"The transfer is rejected with a clear error indicating KYC verification is required"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An unverified account","when":"Another user sends $300 to this account","then":"The transfer succeeds and the balance increases by $300"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A verified account that has already sent $4,800 today","when":"The account holder attempts to send $300","then":"The transfer is rejected because it would exceed the $5,000 daily limit"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A verified account that has already sent $4,800 today","when":"The account holder attempts to send $200","then":"The transfer succeeds (cumulative $5,000 equals the limit)"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A partially-verified account that has sent $0 today","when":"The account holder attempts to send $501","then":"The transfer is rejected because it exceeds the $500 daily limit"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A transaction initiated yesterday that has reached T+1 settlement","when":"The sender requests a reversal","then":"The reversal is rejected with a message indicating the transaction is settled and irreversible"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A transaction initiated 2 hours ago (pre-settlement)","when":"The sender requests a reversal","then":"The reversal is permitted and the funds are returned"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A verified account attempting to send $100","when":"The recipient is on the sanctions list","then":"The transfer is blocked before any funds move, and the event is logged for compliance audit"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A user account in normal status","when":"The user enters incorrect credentials 5 consecutive times","then":"The account is locked and further login attempts are rejected even with the correct password"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A user account that has been locked due to failed logins","when":"The user attempts to reset their password or waits 24 hours","then":"The account remains locked — only a support agent unlock action restores access"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A verified account sending $1,001 to another user","when":"The transaction is initiated","then":"A notification is sent to the account holder about the transaction"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A verified account sending $999 to another user","when":"The transaction is initiated","then":"No high-value transaction notification is triggered"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A user who has failed login 4 times consecutively","when":"The user logs in successfully on the 5th attempt","then":"The failed-attempt counter resets to zero and the account is not locked"},{"id":"AC-14","given":"A verified account attempting to send funds to a sanctioned entity","when":"The sanctions list is checked","then":"The transfer is blocked regardless of the amount, verification tier, or daily limit remaining"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Partially-verified account attempts to withdraw funds","expected_behavior":"The spec says unverified accounts cannot withdraw but is silent on partially-verified withdrawal. This needs clarification — conservatively, partially-verified should be treated as unable to withdraw until full KYC is complete."},{"scenario":"Transaction amount is exactly $1,000","expected_behavior":"The notification rule says 'over $1,000' — a transaction of exactly $1,000.00 should NOT trigger a notification. Only amounts strictly greater than $1,000."},{"scenario":"Daily limit boundary — does 'daily' mean rolling 24 hours or calendar day?","expected_behavior":"Spec says 'daily' without defining the window. This affects whether a user can send $5,000 at 11:59 PM and another $5,000 at 12:01 AM. Needs product clarification."},{"scenario":"Sender is verified but recipient becomes sanctioned mid-settlement (between T+0 and T+1)","expected_behavior":"The sanctions check must occur at initiation AND at settlement. If the recipient is sanctioned at settlement time, the transaction should be frozen and escalated to compliance — not settled."},{"scenario":"Multiple concurrent transfers that individually are within the daily limit but collectively exceed it","expected_behavior":"The daily limit check must be atomic or use a reservation pattern. Two simultaneous $3,000 transfers from a verified account must not both succeed."},{"scenario":"Account receives money while locked","expected_behavior":"Account lock is a login/access control — inbound transfers to a locked account should still succeed since the sender initiated them. The locked user simply cannot access the funds until unlocked."},{"scenario":"Self-transfer (user sends money to themselves)","expected_behavior":"Should still count toward the daily transfer limit and be subject to sanctions screening on both ends."},{"scenario":"Transaction of exactly $5,000 from a verified account with $0 sent today","expected_behavior":"Should succeed — the limit is $5,000, and $5,000 equals the limit. The rule is 'up to,' not 'less than.'"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect daily limit enforcement could lead to either lost transaction revenue (limits too strict) or regulatory fines (limits too lax). Sanctions violations carry fines of $250K–$1M+ per incident under OFAC. Settlement reversal bugs could cause direct financial loss.","operational":"KYC tier management adds operational complexity. Support team must handle account unlocks manually — high lock rates could overwhelm support. Sanctions list must be kept current (OFAC updates frequently). Settlement finality requires clear cutoff processes.","user":"Tiered verification creates friction for new users who want to send money immediately. Account lockout after 5 attempts may frustrate legitimate users with forgotten passwords. Notification for >$1,000 builds trust and transparency.","risk":"Highest risks are sanctions compliance (legal/criminal liability) and settlement irreversibility (financial integrity). Secondary risks include brute-force attacks if lockout is bypassed and money laundering if KYC is circumvented. The absence of rate limiting beyond login (e.g., transaction velocity) is a gap.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"End Users (Wallet Holders)","impact":"Verification requirements gate access to send/withdraw. Lockout policy affects accessibility. Notifications provide transparency on large transactions."},{"group":"Compliance / Legal Team","impact":"Sanctions screening and KYC enforcement are their direct responsibility. Failures here create personal liability for compliance officers."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Manual account unlock requirement creates workload. Must be trained on when/how to unlock and how to verify caller identity before unlocking."},{"group":"Finance / Treasury","impact":"Settlement finality (T+1) and daily limits directly affect cash flow management and reconciliation processes."},{"group":"Fraud / Risk Team","impact":"Login lockout is their first-line defense. Daily limits constrain fraud exposure. They need audit logs of sanctions blocks and failed login attempts."},{"group":"Regulators (FinCEN, OFAC, State MSB regulators)","impact":"KYC, sanctions screening, and transaction limits are regulatory requirements. Audit trail completeness is essential for examinations."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is clear on most rules but has several ambiguities: the definition of 'daily' (rolling vs calendar), partially-verified withdrawal rights, whether sanctions screening occurs only at initiation or also at settlement, and the exact meaning of 'partially-verified' vs 'unverified' for send capabilities. The core rules are unambiguous and well-defined. No contradictions detected. Deducting confidence for the gaps identified.","high_confidence_areas":["KYC requirement for sending funds","Settlement irreversibility at T+1","Sanctions blocking requirement","Account lockout after 5 failed attempts","Notification threshold at >$1,000"],"low_confidence_areas":["Definition of 'daily' window for transfer limits (rolling 24h vs calendar day)","Partially-verified account withdrawal rights (spec is silent)","Sanctions screening timing relative to settlement window","Whether 'send money' in BR-1 applies to partially-verified accounts (spec implies yes with $500 limit, but BR-1 says KYC must be complete)"],"assumptions":["Partially-verified accounts CAN send (up to $500/day) since a limit is specified for them, meaning BR-1's 'complete identity verification' encompasses partial verification as a tier that permits limited sending","'Daily' means calendar day in the platform's configured timezone","The $1,000 notification threshold is strictly greater-than, not greater-than-or-equal","Sanctions lists are maintained externally and the system consumes them — list curation is out of scope"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec states 'Users must complete identity verification (KYC) before they can send money' but then assigns a $500 daily limit to 'partially-verified accounts.' These two statements are in tension: if KYC must be complete to send, partially-verified users should not be able to send. But a limit is specified, implying they can. Clarify: does 'complete KYC' mean 'reach at least partial verification' or 'reach full verification'?","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Compliance Team","Product Management"],"options":["A: Partially-verified can send up to $500/day (KYC = at least partial)","B: Only fully verified can send (partially-verified is treated like unverified for sending)"],"recommendation":"Option A — the existence of a $500 limit for partially-verified accounts strongly implies they are intended to send. Recommend clarifying the spec to say 'Users must reach at least partial verification to send money.'"},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Daily transfer limit' does not define whether 'daily' means a rolling 24-hour window or a calendar-day reset. This significantly affects limit enforcement at day boundaries.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Finance Team","Fraud/Risk Team"],"options":["A: Rolling 24-hour window from first transaction","B: Calendar day reset at midnight in platform timezone"],"recommendation":"Option B (calendar day) — simpler to implement, easier for users to understand, and industry-standard for consumer wallets. Specify the timezone."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"The spec does not address whether partially-verified accounts can withdraw funds. It only says unverified accounts cannot withdraw. This is a gap that affects the user journey for partially-verified users.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Product Management","Compliance Team"],"options":["Allow withdrawal with reduced limits for partially-verified","Block withdrawal until full verification"],"recommendation":"Block withdrawal until full verification — withdrawal to external accounts carries higher fraud/AML risk than internal transfers."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Digital wallet platform enabling peer-to-peer money transfers with tiered identity verification, regulatory compliance (KYC/AML), and account security controls. The product operates in a regulated financial services space where compliance failures carry legal and financial penalties.","bounded_context":"Digital Wallet — Transfers & Account Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"KYC (Know Your Customer)","definition":"Identity verification process that
